1: From nasa'; a burden; specifically, tribute, or porterage; figuratively, an utterance, chiefly a doom, especially singing; mental, desire.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
My messenger, an Isr. prophet. From the same as mal'ak; ministrative; Malaki, a prophet.
2: To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Apparently a form of the passive participle of asah in the original sense of handling; rough; Esav, a son of Isaac, including his posterity.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.

To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
Devastation, waste. Or shimamah; feminine of shamem; devastation; figuratively, astonishment.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
From nachal; properly, something inherited, i.e. occupancy, or an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
A female jackal. Probably feminine of tan; a female jackal.
The name of a condiment. Or oedowm; from 'adom; red; Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob; hence the region occupied by him.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To beat down, shatter. A primitive root; to demolish.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
To build. A primitive root; to build.
Waste, desolation, ruin. Feminine of choreb; properly, drought, i.e. a desolation.
Thus, here. From the prefix k and huw'; properly, like this, i.e. By implication, thus; also here; or now.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To build. A primitive root; to build.
To throw down, break or tear down. A primitive root; to pull down or in pieces, break, &? Destroy.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Border, boundary, territory. Or gbul; from gabal; properly, a cord, i.e. a boundary; by extens. The territory inclosed.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To be indignant. A primitive root; properly, to foam at the mouth, i.e. To be enraged.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Border, boundary, territory. Or gbul; from gabal; properly, a cord, i.e. a boundary; by extens. The territory inclosed.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.

Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).

An altar. From zabach; an altar.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

A door. From dalah; something swinging, i.e. The valve of a door.

Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.

10: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.

Why? for what reason?. Or madduaa; from mah and the passive participle of yada'; what known?; i.e. why?.
To act or deal treacherously. A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
A primitive root; properly, to bore, i.e. to wound, to dissolve; figuratively, to profane, to break, to begin; denom. to play.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
To act or deal treacherously. A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.

To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
To marry, rule over. A primitive root; to be master; hence, to marry.
That which is foreign, foreignness. From nakar; foreign, or a foreigner, or heathendom.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.

To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.

To rouse oneself, awake. A primitive root; to wake.
From an unused root meaning to apportion, i.e. Bestow; a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.

Second (an ord. number). From shanah; properly, double, i.e. Second; also adverbially, again.

Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.

Lord. Or adon; from an unused root; sovereign, i.e. Controller.

Suddenness, suddenly. Or pith.om; from petha'; instantly.

To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.

To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Possession, property. Feminine passive participle of an unused root meaning to shut up; wealth.
To spare. A primitive root; to commiserate; by implication, to spare.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To spare. A primitive root; to commiserate; by implication, to spare.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To work, serve. A primitive root; to work; by implication, to serve, till, enslave, etc.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To work, serve. A primitive root; to work; by implication, to serve, till, enslave, etc.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To work, serve. A primitive root; to work; by implication, to serve, till, enslave, etc.

1: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
To kindle, consume, to be, brutish. A primitive root; to kindle, i.e. Consume; also to be(-come) brutish.
(portable) stove, firepot. From niyr; a fire-pot.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
when I will set them ablaze,”
To blaze up, flame. A primitive root; properly, to lick, i.e. to blaze.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A root. From sharash; a root.
A branch, bough. From an unused root meaning to cover; a twig.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
Fearing, reverent. From yare'; fearing; morally, reverent.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
From an unused root meaning to be brilliant; the sun; by implication, the east; figuratively, a ray, i.e. A notched battlement.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
To rise, come forth. A primitive root; properly, to irradiate, i.e. To rise; specifically, to appear.
From rapha'; properly, curative, i.e. Literally a medicine, or a cure; figuratively deliverance, or placidity.
Wing, extremity. From kanaph; an edge or extremity; specifically a wing, a flap, a quarter, a pinnacle.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Probably to spring about. A primitive root; to spread; figuratively, act proudly.
A calf. From the same as agol; a calf, especially one nearly grown.
A stall. From an unused root meaning to tie up; a stall.
To press, crush, tread down. A primitive root; to squeeze out juice; figuratively, to trample.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Ashes. From an unused root meaning to bestrew; ashes.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
4: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
A great Isr. leader, prophet and lawgiver. From mashah; drawing out, i.e. Rescued; Mosheh, the Israelite lawgiver.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
To lay charge (upon), give charge (to), command, order. A primitive root; to constitute, enjoin.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Waste, a mountain in Sinai. From charab; desolate; Choreb, a name for the Sinaitic mountains.
5: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Or prolonged teliyahuw; from 'el and Yahh; God of Jehovah; Elijah, the name of the famous prophet and of two other Israelites.
A spokesman, speaker, prophet. From naba'; a prophet or inspired man.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To turn back, in, to retreat, again
